text: Tristan Harris introduced the concept of 'time well spent' as an alternative design philosophy to engagement-maximizing metrics. The conventional measure of product success — time on screen — treats all minutes as equal. Harris argued that ten minutes absorbed in a meaningful conversation is categorically different from ten minutes trapped in a compulsive scroll. He proposed that platforms audit their features against users' own stated values: if people consistently report regretting a behavior after doing it, that is evidence the design is working against them. The distinction is between engagement that the user chose and engagement the interface manufactured.
question: What test does Harris propose for whether a platform feature works against users?

options.text: Whether users consistently report regretting the behavior after doing it
Whether the feature generates more time on screen than comparable apps
Whether the feature was designed primarily to serve advertiser interests
Whether users can accurately estimate how much time they spend on it
